004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.lang.reflect.*;
023import java.time.*;
024import java.time.chrono.*;
025import java.time.format.*;
026import java.time.temporal.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.concurrent.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swaps.*;
032
033/**
034 * Supported wire formats for {@link TemporalAccessor} values.
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#temporalFormat(TemporalFormat)},
038 * {@link Marshalled#temporalFormat()},
039 * {@link MarshalledProp#temporalFormat()}, and
040 * {@link MarshalledConfig#temporalFormat()} to control how
041 * {@link TemporalAccessor} values ({@link Instant}, {@link LocalDate}, {@link LocalDateTime},
042 * {@link LocalTime}, {@link OffsetDateTime}, {@link OffsetTime}, {@link ZonedDateTime}, {@link Year},
043 * {@link YearMonth}, {@link MonthDay}, …) are written to and read from the wire.
044 *
045 * <p>
046 * The default is {@link #DEFAULT} which uses the per-subtype default formatter
047 * (e.g. {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_INSTANT} for {@link Instant}, {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE}
048 * for {@link LocalDate}, etc.) — preserving the historical wire output produced before this enum existed.
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Numeric formats ({@link #MILLIS}) emit native numeric wire types on binary serializers
052 * (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack) and bare numbers on text formats. Text formats emit a quoted ISO 8601 string for
053 * every other constant.
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * Most subtypes implement {@link Temporal}, but {@link MonthDay} only implements {@link TemporalAccessor}.
057 * Format/parse methods accept any {@link TemporalAccessor} so that {@link MonthDay} round-trips through the
058 * same dispatch path as the {@link Temporal} subtypes.
059 */
060public enum TemporalFormat {
061
062   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
063   NOT_SET,
064
065   /**
066    * Per-subtype default formatter (the historical wire output).
067    *
068    * <p>
069    * Uses the ISO formatter most appropriate for each {@link Temporal} subtype:
070    * {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_INSTANT} for {@link Instant}, {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE}
071    * for {@link LocalDate}, {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME} for {@link ZonedDateTime} /
072    * {@link OffsetDateTime}, etc.
073    */
074   DEFAULT,
075
076   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#BASIC_ISO_DATE}: <js>"20111203"</js>. */
077   BASIC_ISO_DATE,
078
079   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03+01:00"</js> or <js>"2011-12-03"</js>. */
080   ISO_DATE,
081
082   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00[Europe/Paris]"</js>. */
083   ISO_DATE_TIME,
084
085   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_INSTANT}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30Z"</js>. */
086   ISO_INSTANT,
087
088   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03"</js>. */
089   ISO_LOCAL_DATE,
090
091   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30"</js>. */
092   ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME,
093
094   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_LOCAL_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30"</js>. */
095   ISO_LOCAL_TIME,
096
097   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_DATE}: <js>"2011-12-03+01:00"</js>. */
098   ISO_OFFSET_DATE,
099
100   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00"</js>. */
101   ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME,
102
103   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_OFFSET_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30+01:00"</js>. */
104   ISO_OFFSET_TIME,
105
106   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_ORDINAL_DATE}: <js>"2012-337"</js>. */
107   ISO_ORDINAL_DATE,
108
109   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_TIME}: <js>"10:15:30+01:00"</js> or <js>"10:15:30"</js>. */
110   ISO_TIME,
111
112   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_WEEK_DATE}: <js>"2012-W48-6"</js>. */
113   ISO_WEEK_DATE,
114
115   /** Year only — pattern {@code "uuuu"}: <js>"2012"</js>. */
116   ISO_YEAR,
117
118   /** Year and month — pattern {@code "uuuu-MM"}: <js>"2012-12"</js>. */
119   ISO_YEAR_MONTH,
120
121   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME}: <js>"2011-12-03T10:15:30+01:00[Europe/Paris]"</js>. */
122   ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME,
123
124   /** {@link DateTimeFormatter#RFC_1123_DATE_TIME}: <js>"Tue, 3 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT"</js>. */
125   RFC_1123_DATE_TIME,
126
127   /**
128    * Epoch milliseconds as a numeric value.
129    *
130    * <p>
131    * Emitted as a native int64 by binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack) and as a bare integer
132    * literal by text serializers.
133    *
134    * <p>
135    * <b>Per-subtype semantics (midnight UTC for non-instant subtypes):</b>
136    * <ul>
137    *   <li>{@link Instant} — {@code instant.toEpochMilli()}.
138    *   <li>{@link OffsetDateTime}, {@link ZonedDateTime} — {@code value.toInstant().toEpochMilli()}.
139    *   <li>{@link LocalDateTime} — {@code ldt.toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochMilli()}.
140    *   <li>{@link LocalDate} — {@code ld.atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli()} (midnight UTC).
141    *   <li>{@link YearMonth} — first day of the month at midnight UTC.
142    *   <li>{@link Year} — January 1 at midnight UTC.
143    *   <li>{@link LocalTime}, {@link OffsetTime}, {@link MonthDay} — fall back to the type's {@link #DEFAULT}
144    *       (ISO string form) because no defensible epoch-millis interpretation exists without an associated
145    *       date/year.  See {@link #isMillisNumeric(Class)} for the canonical decision predicate.
146    * </ul>
147    *
148    * <p>
149    * <b>The asymmetry is intentional:</b> a configured {@code MILLIS} setting silently produces a string
150    * for {@link LocalTime} / {@link OffsetTime} / {@link MonthDay} and a number for every other subtype.
151    */
152   MILLIS;
153
154   private static final ZoneId Z = ZoneId.of("Z");
155
156   private static final DateTimeFormatter YEAR_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("uuuu");
157   private static final DateTimeFormatter YEAR_MONTH_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("uuuu-MM");
158
159   private static final Map<Class<?>, DateTimeFormatter> DEFAULT_FORMATTERS = Map.ofEntries(
160      Map.entry(Instant.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT),
161      Map.entry(ZonedDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME),
162      Map.entry(OffsetDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME),
163      Map.entry(LocalDate.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE),
164      Map.entry(LocalDateTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME),
165      Map.entry(LocalTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_TIME),
166      Map.entry(OffsetTime.class, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_TIME),
167      Map.entry(Year.class, YEAR_FORMATTER),
168      Map.entry(YearMonth.class, YEAR_MONTH_FORMATTER)
169   );
170
171   private static final Map<Class<? extends TemporalAccessor>, Method> FROM_METHODS = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
172
173   /**
174    * Returns <jk>true</jk> when {@link #MILLIS} produces a numeric wire string for the specified subtype.
175    *
176    * <p>
177    * Single source of truth for the {@code MILLIS} wire-type decision shared by {@link #format} and the
178    * marshalling-side {@code temporalSwap} / {@code temporalAccessorSwap} factories.  The helper exists so
179    * the swap-side {@code Long.valueOf(s)} coercion only fires when {@link #format} actually emitted a
180    * numeric string — a mismatch on either side is a {@link NumberFormatException} waiting to happen.
181    *
182    * <p>
183    * Returns <jk>false</jk> for {@link LocalTime}, {@link OffsetTime}, and {@link MonthDay} — these
184    * subtypes have no defensible epoch-millis interpretation, so {@link #format}'s {@code MILLIS} branch
185    * falls back to {@link #DEFAULT} (an ISO string) for them and the swap must keep the value as a string.
186    *
187    * @param c The {@link TemporalAccessor} subtype to query.
188    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@link #format} emits a {@link Long}-parseable numeric string for {@code c}
189    *         under {@link #MILLIS}; <jk>false</jk> if the {@code MILLIS} branch falls back to ISO string.
190    */
191   public static boolean isMillisNumeric(Class<? extends TemporalAccessor> c) {
192      return ! (LocalTime.class.equals(c) || OffsetTime.class.equals(c) || MonthDay.class.equals(c));
193   }
194
195   /**
196    * Formats the specified value using this format.
197    *
198    * @param value The value to format. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
199    * @param zoneId The default zone id used for time-only / unzoned values.
200    * @return The formatted string, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
201    *         For {@link #MILLIS} this is a numeric string for every subtype except {@link LocalTime},
202    *         {@link OffsetTime}, and {@link MonthDay} (which fall back to their {@link #DEFAULT} ISO
203    *         string form).
204    */
205   @SuppressWarnings({
206      "java:S3776" // Per-subtype MILLIS coercion logic is intentionally explicit; flattening hurts readability
207   })
208   public String format(TemporalAccessor value, ZoneId zoneId) {
209      if (value == null)
210         return null;
211      // MonthDay only implements TemporalAccessor (not Temporal) and has no DateTimeFormatter that
212      // matches its --MM-DD wire form natively.  Use its own toString/parse pair regardless of the
213      // configured format — every other TemporalFormat value is structurally meaningless without a year.
214      if (value instanceof MonthDay md)
215         return md.toString();
216      if (this == MILLIS) {
217         Long millis = toEpochMillis(value);
218         if (millis != null)
219            return Long.toString(millis);
220         return DEFAULT.format(value, zoneId);
221      }
222      var zone = zoneId == null ? ZoneId.systemDefault() : zoneId;
223      var tc = value.getClass();
224      if (!DEFAULT_FORMATTERS.containsKey(tc) && (this == DEFAULT || this == NOT_SET))
225         return DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(toInstant(value, zone));
226      var coerced = convertForFormatter(value, zone);
227      return formatter(tc).format(coerced);
228   }
229
230   private static Instant toInstant(TemporalAccessor value, ZoneId zone) {
231      if (value instanceof Instant i)
232         return i;
233      try {
234         return Instant.from(value);
235      } catch (Exception e) {
236         if (value instanceof ChronoLocalDate cld)
237            return cld.atTime(LocalTime.MIDNIGHT).atZone(zone).toInstant();
238         if (value instanceof ChronoLocalDateTime<?> cldt)
239            return cldt.atZone(zone).toInstant();
240         if (value instanceof ChronoZonedDateTime<?> czdt)
241            return czdt.toInstant();
242         throw e;
243      }
244   }
245
246   /**
247    * Parses the specified wire value using this format.
248    *
249    * @param <T> The temporal subtype.
250    * @param value The wire value. Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
251    * @param targetType The target temporal subtype.  Must be either a {@link Temporal} subtype or
252    *                   {@link MonthDay} (the only non-{@link Temporal} {@link TemporalAccessor} the JDK
253    *                   currently exposes).
254    * @param zoneId The default zone id used for time-only / unzoned values.
255    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
256    */
257   @SuppressWarnings({
258      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked cast on reflective from() result
259      "java:S3776" // Per-subtype MILLIS dispatch is intentionally explicit; flattening hurts readability
260   })
261   public <T extends TemporalAccessor> T parse(String value, Class<T> targetType, ZoneId zoneId) {
262      if (value == null)
263         return null;
264      var s = value.trim();
265      if (s.isEmpty())
266         return null;
267      var zone = zoneId == null ? ZoneId.systemDefault() : zoneId;
268      if (this == MILLIS) {
269         // MonthDay reverses what format() emits at MILLIS — its native --MM-DD shape; numeric input
270         // at MILLIS×MonthDay is unreachable on the round-trip path because format() emits a string.
271         if (MonthDay.class.equals(targetType))
272            return (T) MonthDay.parse(s);
273         // LocalTime never had a defensible epoch-millis interpretation; legacy callers get DEFAULT.parse.
274         if (LocalTime.class.equals(targetType))
275            return DEFAULT.parse(s, targetType, zone);
276         // OffsetTime: format() falls back to DEFAULT (ISO_OFFSET_TIME string).  Be lenient on parse —
277         // route numeric input through fromEpochMillis (legacy lenient support) and route the
278         // ISO_OFFSET_TIME wire form back through DEFAULT.parse so the round-trip closes.
279         if (OffsetTime.class.equals(targetType) && ! isAllDigits(s))
280            return DEFAULT.parse(s, targetType, zone);
281         return fromEpochMillis(Long.parseLong(s), targetType, zone);
282      }
283      // MonthDay's --MM-DD wire shape is its only stable representation; route to its native parser
284      // regardless of the configured format.
285      if (MonthDay.class.equals(targetType))
286         return (T) MonthDay.parse(s);
287      // Lenient: epoch-millis numeric strings are accepted for DEFAULT/NOT_SET on instant-like subtypes only.
288      // Date-only subtypes (Year, YearMonth, LocalDate, MonthDay, LocalTime) use ISO patterns that are themselves
289      // numeric ("2012", "2012-12") and must not be reinterpreted as epoch millis.
290      if ((this == DEFAULT || this == NOT_SET) && isAllDigits(s) && isInstantLike(targetType))
291         return fromEpochMillis(Long.parseLong(s), targetType, zone);
292      var offset = (targetType == Instant.class) ? Z : zone;
293      var ta = new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(formatter(targetType).parse(s), offset);
294      return (T) invokeFrom(targetType, ta);
295   }
296
297   private static boolean isInstantLike(Class<?> targetType) {
298      return Instant.class.equals(targetType)
299         || ZonedDateTime.class.equals(targetType)
300         || OffsetDateTime.class.equals(targetType)
301         || LocalDateTime.class.equals(targetType);
302   }
303
304   private static boolean isAllDigits(String s) {
305      var n = s.length();
306      if (n == 0)
307         return false;
308      var i = 0;
309      if (s.charAt(0) == '-' || s.charAt(0) == '+') {
310         if (n == 1)
311            return false;
312         i = 1;
313      }
314      for (; i < n; i++)
315         if (!Character.isDigit(s.charAt(i)))
316            return false;
317      return true;
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format is numeric on the wire.
322    *
323    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a numeric wire value.
324    */
325   public boolean isNumeric() {
326      return this == MILLIS;
327   }
328
329   private static DateTimeFormatter defaultFormatter(Class<?> tc) {
330      var f = DEFAULT_FORMATTERS.get(tc);
331      return f != null ? f : DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT;
332   }
333
334   @SuppressWarnings({
335      "java:S1301" // switch over enum: explicit case constants keep the per-format mapping legible
336   })
337   private DateTimeFormatter formatter(Class<?> tc) {
338      return switch (this) {
339         case NOT_SET, DEFAULT -> defaultFormatter(tc);
340         case BASIC_ISO_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.BASIC_ISO_DATE;
341         case ISO_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE;
342         case ISO_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE_TIME;
343         case ISO_INSTANT -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT;
344         case ISO_LOCAL_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE;
345         case ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME;
346         case ISO_LOCAL_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_TIME;
347         case ISO_OFFSET_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE;
348         case ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME;
349         case ISO_OFFSET_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_TIME;
350         case ISO_ORDINAL_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_ORDINAL_DATE;
351         case ISO_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_TIME;
352         case ISO_WEEK_DATE -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_WEEK_DATE;
353         case ISO_YEAR -> YEAR_FORMATTER;
354         case ISO_YEAR_MONTH -> YEAR_MONTH_FORMATTER;
355         case ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.ISO_ZONED_DATE_TIME;
356         case RFC_1123_DATE_TIME -> DateTimeFormatter.RFC_1123_DATE_TIME;
357         case MILLIS -> throw new IllegalStateException("MILLIS does not use a DateTimeFormatter");
358      };
359   }
360
361   /**
362    * Returns whether the configured formatter accepts a missing zone, mirroring the historical
363    * {@code TemporalSwap.zoneOptional()} flag.
364    */
365   @SuppressWarnings({
366      "java:S6541" // exhaustive switch keeps the per-format zone-optional table explicit
367   })
368   private boolean zoneOptional() {
369      return switch (this) {
370         case BASIC_ISO_DATE, ISO_DATE, ISO_DATE_TIME, ISO_LOCAL_DATE, ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME, ISO_LOCAL_TIME,
371            ISO_ORDINAL_DATE, ISO_TIME, ISO_WEEK_DATE, ISO_YEAR, ISO_YEAR_MONTH -> true;
372         default -> false;
373      };
374   }
375
376   @SuppressWarnings({
377      "java:S3776" // Per-subtype coercion table mirrors the legacy TemporalSwap.convertToSerializable path
378   })
379   private Temporal convertForFormatter(TemporalAccessor value, ZoneId zoneId) {
380      if (value instanceof Temporal t && (this == DEFAULT || this == NOT_SET))
381         return t;
382      var tc = value.getClass();
383      // Instant always serializes in GMT.
384      if (tc == Instant.class)
385         return ZonedDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(value, Z));
386      // Zoned / offset variants are already serializable by any pattern.
387      if (value instanceof Temporal t && (tc == ZonedDateTime.class || tc == OffsetDateTime.class))
388         return t;
389      if (zoneOptional()) {
390         if (value instanceof LocalDateTime ldt)
391            return ldt;
392         if (tc == OffsetTime.class)
393            return ZonedDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(value, zoneId));
394         return LocalDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(value, zoneId));
395      }
396      return ZonedDateTime.from(new DefaultingTemporalAccessor(value, zoneId));
397   }
398
399   @SuppressWarnings({
400      "java:S3776" // Per-subtype epoch-millis coercion is intentionally explicit
401   })
402   private static Long toEpochMillis(TemporalAccessor value) {
403      if (value instanceof Instant i)
404         return i.toEpochMilli();
405      if (value instanceof OffsetDateTime odt)
406         return odt.toInstant().toEpochMilli();
407      if (value instanceof ZonedDateTime zdt)
408         return zdt.toInstant().toEpochMilli();
409      if (value instanceof LocalDateTime ldt)
410         return ldt.toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochMilli();
411      if (value instanceof LocalDate ld)
412         return ld.atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli();
413      if (value instanceof YearMonth ym)
414         return ym.atDay(1).atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli();
415      if (value instanceof Year y)
416         return y.atDay(1).atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli();
417      return null;
418   }
419
420   @SuppressWarnings({
421      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts for per-subtype factory dispatch
422   })
423   private static <T extends TemporalAccessor> T fromEpochMillis(long millis, Class<T> targetType, ZoneId zoneId) {
424      var instant = Instant.ofEpochMilli(millis);
425      if (targetType == Instant.class) return (T) instant;
426      if (targetType == ZonedDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId);
427      if (targetType == OffsetDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toOffsetDateTime();
428      if (targetType == LocalDateTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC).toLocalDateTime();
429      if (targetType == LocalDate.class) return (T) instant.atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC).toLocalDate();
430      if (targetType == OffsetTime.class) return (T) instant.atZone(zoneId).toOffsetDateTime().toOffsetTime();
431      if (targetType == YearMonth.class) return (T) YearMonth.from(instant.atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC));
432      if (targetType == Year.class) return (T) Year.from(instant.atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC));
433      throw new IllegalArgumentException("Cannot convert epoch millis to " + targetType.getName());
434   }
435
436   private static Method findFromMethod(Class<? extends TemporalAccessor> tc) {
437      // @formatter:off
438      return FROM_METHODS.computeIfAbsent(tc, k -> info(tc).getPublicMethod(
439         x -> x.isStatic()
440            && x.isNotDeprecated()
441            && x.hasName("from")
442            && x.hasReturnType(tc)
443            && x.hasParameterTypes(TemporalAccessor.class)
444         )
445         .map(MethodInfo::inner)
446         .orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalArgumentException("No static from(TemporalAccessor) on " + tc.getName())));
447      // @formatter:on
448   }
449
450   private static Object invokeFrom(Class<? extends TemporalAccessor> tc, TemporalAccessor ta) {
451      try {
452         return findFromMethod(tc).invoke(null, ta);
453      } catch (IllegalAccessException | InvocationTargetException e) {
454         throw toRex(e);
455      }
456   }
457}
